title: Repeater adverts only during night setting

messages.author: Heronimo
messages.text: In the Dutch Mesh there is a lot of traffic during daytime. This leads to very high airtime utilization. A large part driven by flood adverts. Would it be possible to create a setting that restricts repeater adverts to a random moment within a quiet timeslot, for example between 0.00h and 6.00h? That would free up airtime during more busy times.
messages.content_html: In the Dutch Mesh there is a lot of traffic during daytime. This leads to very high airtime utilization. A large part driven by flood adverts. Would it be possible to create a setting that restricts repeater adverts to a random moment within a quiet timeslot, for example between 0.00h and 6.00h? That would free up airtime during more busy times.
messages.reactions_html:
3
1
1

messages.author: Beylix
messages.text: Would be good start but preferably I think it should automatically take into account if air is busy If there ever would be a disaster at night we wouldn't want adverts to get in the way
messages.content_html: Would be good start but preferably I think it should automatically take into account if air is busy If there ever would be a disaster at night we wouldn't want adverts to get in the way

messages.author: Beylix
messages.text: Most have their clock set so it would help already
messages.content_html: Most have their clock set so it would help already

messages.author: Heronimo
messages.text: And actually we think flood adverts for stationary repeaters would only need to happen once a week or so in a stable grid. So a few hours every night would leave more than ample time to spread it out.
messages.content_html: And actually we think flood adverts for stationary repeaters would only need to happen once a week or so in a stable grid. So a few hours every night would leave more than ample time to spread it out.

messages.author: wesselah
messages.text: Technically flood adverts are not needed at all but people want/ need them to relate to the mesh. A lot of other solutions are doable to restrict adverts . Just to mention a couple TTL with hop count adjusted to mesh size, restrict advert at regions would be a way, , adding jitter to delay forwarding against time , Probabilistic flooding adding a change of flooding being forwarded or not . At the rate the mesh is crowing ( 16-12-2025 630 repeaters) in the netherlands this mesh will selfdestruct if it hits 1200 repeaters a doubeling in nodes is a tripeling in adverts With 630 nodes we are talking give or take 400.000 advert floods every x hours . Every change suggested in this thread needs support off the meshcore team to look into. Please take this seriously or we are following the meshtastic path too the graveyard where everybody is collecting icons off people and repeaters. But no message can travel anymore. Today we had clear propogation on the rf side so germany connected over a period of 1 hour almost 20 new repeaters came in the discovered list .

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: I submitted a very simple PR, see above. The issue is that people like "things popping up" in their companion and I understand that. We had some really good radio weather in Germany the last couple of days and it already gave a glimpse into the future what happens if many local city meshes get connected all of a sudden. Try to reach the couple of companion owner who set their companions to hourly flood adverts. Or the repeater or room server owner who thinks it's absolutely vital to hammer the mesh with a flood advert every hour. Trying to tell people to up the time between flood adverts to 48h is not working well. I am curios how you guys are dealing with advert traffic in the puget mesh? (edited)
messages.content_html: I submitted a very simple PR, see above. The issue is that people like "things popping up" in their companion and I understand that. We had some really good radio weather in Germany the last couple of days and it already gave a glimpse into the future what happens if many local city meshes get connected all of a sudden. Try to reach the couple of companion owner who set their companions to hourly flood adverts. Or the repeater or room server owner who thinks it's absolutely vital to hammer the mesh with a flood advert every hour. Trying to tell people to up the time between flood adverts to 48h is not working well. I am curios how you guys are dealing with advert traffic in the puget mesh? (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

Cisien
You can also contribute solutions

messages.author: Heronimo
messages.text: Yes, doing some congestion management baked into the firmware would help. Doing it on RX airtime utilization would automatically shift most adverts to calmer moments which was the exact point of this topic. And it would also allow more frequent adverts in a small mesh that's just starting up.
messages.content_html: Yes, doing some congestion management baked into the firmware would help. Doing it on RX airtime utilization would automatically shift most adverts to calmer moments which was the exact point of this topic. And it would also allow more frequent adverts in a small mesh that's just starting up.
messages.reply_html:
Cisien
i think the right first step approach is to make advert interval dependent on rx airtime utilization

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: To me that only makes sense in combination with flood limiting (aka TTL). Mesh networking to my understanding is focused on regional (e.g. ity wide) use. Getting flood adverts from repeaters 100s of miles away is pointless, just to put the on somebody's map. Then, additionally to that limit the flooding of this regional mesh by decreasing advert frequency and RX airtime utilization check. So in short: time to live for flood advert packets (e.g. <10 hops) lower advert frequency (e.g. 24h-->48h) honor RX airtime utilization (edited)
messages.content_html:
To me that only makes sense in combination with flood limiting (aka TTL). Mesh networking to my understanding is focused on regional (e.g. ity wide) use. Getting flood adverts from repeaters 100s of miles away is pointless, just to put the on somebody's map. Then, additionally to that limit the flooding of this regional mesh by decreasing advert frequency and RX airtime utilization check. So in short:
  • time to live for flood advert packets (e.g. <10 hops)
  • lower advert frequency (e.g. 24h-->48h)
  • honor RX airtime utilization
 (edited)

2
1
1

messages.author: ViezeVingertjes
messages.text: What my repeater currently does; is above a airtime (rx+tx) threshold like 30% it simply drops flood adverts. That seems to relieve the pressure on those busy moments. Perhaps it should also better/longer track who it already has seen an advert for, so we limit the amount of flood adverts per identity and enforce it on the repeater side. No need for 2+ flood adverts for the same client in 24h for example. Bet that would cut it down even further.

messages.author: Beanow
messages.text: I think the idea of throttling whether you'll repeat or not is the right idea. Because unlike a fictional scenario where everyone is a bug-free good actor on the latest firmware, throttling as a repeater helps even when only a majority of repeaters are working and up-to-date
messages.content_html: I think the idea of throttling whether you'll repeat or not is the right idea. Because unlike a fictional scenario where everyone is a bug-free good actor on the latest firmware, throttling as a repeater helps even when only a majority of repeaters are working and up-to-date

messages.author: Art-X
messages.text: I saw the youtube about Regions.. that gave me an idea, which could also limit flood adverts if they follow the same pattern, I don't know where to post this, so I also posted this in development: New functionality: Select own Region from a list with all Regions, Flood Ping: repeaters scan direct neighbours and add their main Region their Enable list, Rules: repeater always repeat only in their own enabled regions, ^ means NOT Route example London, Norwich, Harwich, The Hague(NL), Amsterdam HOPS: 0 ^LON:AMS => Still in London, LON will repeat since it's in the enabled region 1 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Border repeater that sees two Regions, only NOR will be repeated, since LON is already in the route list 2 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Repeater with only NOR Repeats NOR, but maintains the route 3 ^LON^NOR^HAR:AMS => Repeater that sees NOR and HAR, will repeat HAR, since NOR is already in the route list 4 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG => Repeater that sees HAR and HAG, will repeat HAG, since HAR is already in the route list 5 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG^AMS:AMS => Repeater that sees HAG and AMS, since the destination AMS is in the routing it will only repeat AMS (edited)Tuesday, 6 January 2026 12:25 Could this also work to reduce flooding and repeat more efficiently? I would also just disable auto flood advert by default and set a minimum of 12 hours. It auto adverts only 1 minute after reboot with default - off. The combination of this might open up a wider network.
messages.content_html: I saw the youtube about Regions.. that gave me an idea, which could also limit flood adverts if they follow the same pattern, I don't know where to post this, so I also posted this in development: New functionality: Select own Region from a list with all Regions, Flood Ping: repeaters scan direct neighbours and add their main Region their Enable list, Rules: repeater always repeat only in their own enabled regions, ^ means NOT Route example London, Norwich, Harwich, The Hague(NL), Amsterdam HOPS: 0 ^LON:AMS => Still in London, LON will repeat since it's in the enabled region 1 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Border repeater that sees two Regions, only NOR will be repeated, since LON is already in the route list 2 ^LON^NOR:AMS => Repeater with only NOR Repeats NOR, but maintains the route 3 ^LON^NOR^HAR:AMS => Repeater that sees NOR and HAR, will repeat HAR, since NOR is already in the route list 4 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG => Repeater that sees HAR and HAG, will repeat HAG, since HAR is already in the route list 5 ^LON^NOR^HAR^HAG^AMS:AMS => Repeater that sees HAG and AMS, since the destination AMS is in the routing it will only repeat AMS (edited)Tuesday, 6 January 2026 12:25 Could this also work to reduce flooding and repeat more efficiently? I would also just disable auto flood advert by default and set a minimum of 12 hours. It auto adverts only 1 minute after reboot with default - off. The combination of this might open up a wider network.

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: The problem with flood adverts is that they are creating a lot of mostly useless traffic, repeated by all other repeaters in a region. Even if the region is kind of small, think of a city with 50-100 repeaters. In my eyes it does not make sense to distribute or limit flood adverts based on regional rules. Adverts should stay local. Very, very local. (edited)
messages.content_html: The problem with flood adverts is that they are creating a lot of mostly useless traffic, repeated by all other repeaters in a region. Even if the region is kind of small, think of a city with 50-100 repeaters. In my eyes it does not make sense to distribute or limit flood adverts based on regional rules. Adverts should stay local. Very, very local. (edited)

The combination of this might open up a wider network.

messages.author: PD4MA Michiel
messages.text: As discussed on the Meshcore NL Telegram, I went to work on being able to selectively configure which packet/advert types get forwarded and setting a max hop for adverts: https://github.com/michielappelman/MeshCore/tree/claude/packet-filter-7fDRU # Enable/disable specific packet type set repeat {req|resp|txt|ack|advert|grp.txt|grp.data|anon|path|trace|multi|control|raw} {on|off} # Enable/disable specific advert sub-type set repeat advert.{none|chat|repeater|room|sensor} {on|off} # Allow adverts from immediate neighbors only set repeat advert.max_hops 1 # Disable hop filtering (default) set repeat advert.max_hops 0 If this is interesting for you, please test it out and share feedback, then we can try to submit a PR (edited)
messages.content_html: As discussed on the Meshcore NL Telegram, I went to work on being able to selectively configure which packet/advert types get forwarded and setting a max hop for adverts: https://github.com/michielappelman/MeshCore/tree/claude/packet-filter-7fDRU # Enable/disable specific packet type set repeat {req|resp|txt|ack|advert|grp.txt|grp.data|anon|path|trace|multi|control|raw} {on|off} # Enable/disable specific advert sub-type set repeat advert.{none|chat|repeater|room|sensor} {on|off} # Allow adverts from immediate neighbors only set repeat advert.max_hops 1 # Disable hop filtering (default) set repeat advert.max_hops 0 If this is interesting for you, please test it out and share feedback, then we can try to submit a PR (edited)

messages.author: PD4MA Michiel
messages.text: I'm not sure the probabilistic forwarding is a good way forward, it inherently makes it less predictable which might confuse/frustrate users. It's easier to say for example: we limit adverts to 5 hops.
messages.content_html: I'm not sure the probabilistic forwarding is a good way forward, it inherently makes it less predictable which might confuse/frustrate users. It's easier to say for example: we limit adverts to 5 hops.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: The probabilistic method serves a greater area without increasing traffic much. That's why it's actually more "performant" than a hard limit. In my eyes this appraoch makes a lot of sense. As the users do not know which packet was dropped I don't know why they should be frustrated. Advert traffic should be mostly local. This is exactly what this algorithm does: 100% for the 1st hop, 31% for the 2nd, 10% for the 3rd, 3% for the 4th. This is much better than setting the hop limit for adverts to 3 and create way more congestion in comparison.
messages.content_html: The probabilistic method serves a greater area without increasing traffic much. That's why it's actually more "performant" than a hard limit. In my eyes this appraoch makes a lot of sense. As the users do not know which packet was dropped I don't know why they should be frustrated. Advert traffic should be mostly local. This is exactly what this algorithm does: 100% for the 1st hop, 31% for the 2nd, 10% for the 3rd, 3% for the 4th. This is much better than setting the hop limit for adverts to 3 and create way more congestion in comparison.
messages.reply_html:
PD4MA Michiel
I'm not sure the probabilistic forwarding is a good way forward, it inherently makes it less predictable which might confuse/frustrate users. It's easier to say for example: we limit adverts to 5 hops.

messages.author: Beanow
messages.text: I'm of the opinion there should be no hard blocks. Turning off a category of packets will make improving the mesh incredibly challenging.
messages.content_html: I'm of the opinion there should be no hard blocks. Turning off a category of packets will make improving the mesh incredibly challenging.

messages.author: mattzz
messages.text: I see where you are coming from and I am also not a fan to provide all possible kinds of kill switches for all packet types. But I think it also depends on the category/packet type. One of MeshCore's big design principles is data minimalism. When it comes to adverts that only serve maps and name resolution of hops but serve absolutely zero technical function for the mesh and on top congest the airwaves and keep repeaters busy I think it is good learn from that. I think automated flood adverts are a big design flaw of the current MeshCore Repeater implementation. We had repeaters in boot loops multiples times in our mesh, spamming the mesh with hundreds of repeaters, spanning multiple hundred kilometers, eating up airtime. Seeing adverts from a couple of hundred kilometers is fascinating, OK - I get that. But that does not mean that there is a working, stable link for "real" payloads. And ironically theses adverts impede the stability and usability of the mesh big time. Up to a point were comunities decided to switch off advert forwarding completely in order to have a working mesh. Though I also don't think path length is a good proxy for "being local". Adverts are debugging tools. They are not required for the mesh to work - at all. They should be very local in order to not keep other repeaters busy dealing with them or stealing airtime from companions because the airwaves around will be busy. I'd suggest to focus much more on trace tools and control packets in order to "debug" the mesh. (edited)
messages.content_html:
I see where you are coming from and I am also not a fan to provide all possible kinds of kill switches for all packet types. But I think it also depends on the category/packet type. One of MeshCore's big design principles is data minimalism. When it comes to adverts that only serve maps and name resolution of hops but serve absolutely zero technical function for the mesh and on top congest the airwaves and keep repeaters busy I think it is good learn from that. I think automated flood adverts are a big design flaw of the current MeshCore Repeater implementation. We had repeaters in boot loops multiples times in our mesh, spamming the mesh with hundreds of repeaters, spanning multiple hundred kilometers, eating up airtime. Seeing adverts from a couple of hundred kilometers is fascinating, OK - I get that. But that does not mean that there is a working, stable link for "real" payloads. And ironically theses adverts impede the stability and usability of the mesh big time. Up to a point were comunities decided to switch off advert forwarding completely in order to have a working mesh.
Though I also don't think path length is a good proxy for "being local".
Adverts are debugging tools. They are not required for the mesh to work - at all. They should be very local in order to not keep other repeaters busy dealing with them or stealing airtime from companions because the airwaves around will be busy. I'd suggest to focus much more on trace tools and control packets in order to "debug" the mesh. (edited)
messages.reply_html:
Beanow
I'm of the opinion there should be no hard blocks. Turning off a category of packets will make improving the mesh incredibly challenging.

messages.author: Beanow
messages.text: Kind of, sort of. New algorithms could make use of the adverts for routing, but indeed today they're using more airtime than they should.
messages.content_html: Kind of, sort of. New algorithms could make use of the adverts for routing, but indeed today they're using more airtime than they should.

messages.author: Stormlove
messages.text: 26.1% of the traffic on west coast mesh this past week was adverts, many of which happen in the middle of day. This number will only increase as more tdecks and repeaters join. (Can we please make tdecks not auto advert by default??) The default advert rate should prob be 24 or 48 hours . Ideally adverts could only happen at night and only if the air utilization is low. If a unit has gps time or a non default time it should prob be considered to have a pretty good clock and could do periodic checks between say 1-7am for low airtime utilization and/or time slots based on its own prefix byte to find a time slot / designated minute to try and send an advert. Just found that the local advert field has a max of only 3 hours, ideally this could much more. We prob want to have this set to 0 to disable it in most cases right? It would be helpful to add some text to the app to let users know this, also kindly asking them via text in the app to have flood adverts of 24 or more hours in busy meshes? (edited)
messages.content_html: 26.1% of the traffic on west coast mesh this past week was adverts, many of which happen in the middle of day. This number will only increase as more tdecks and repeaters join. (Can we please make tdecks not auto advert by default??) The default advert rate should prob be 24 or 48 hours . Ideally adverts could only happen at night and only if the air utilization is low. If a unit has gps time or a non default time it should prob be considered to have a pretty good clock and could do periodic checks between say 1-7am for low airtime utilization and/or time slots based on its own prefix byte to find a time slot / designated minute to try and send an advert. Just found that the local advert field has a max of only 3 hours, ideally this could much more. We prob want to have this set to 0 to disable it in most cases right? It would be helpful to add some text to the app to let users know this, also kindly asking them via text in the app to have flood adverts of 24 or more hours in busy meshes? (edited)
